perturbation.original_text: 1.3 “Delivery” means the completion and/or Delivery of Deliverables in a condition and place acceptable to the Buyer, Acceptance of the Deliverables is not considered to have taken place until payment has been effected.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 “Delivery” means the completion and/or Delivery of Deliverables in a condition and place acceptable to the Buyer. Acceptance of the Deliverables is considered to have taken place immediately upon delivery and inspection by the Buyer, regardless of whether payment has been effected.
perturbation.explanation: By altering the definition of 'Delivery' to include immediate acceptance upon inspection, regardless of payment, it directly contradicts other sections that imply acceptance is contingent on payment. This creates an in-text contradiction, because the original definition of delivery suggests payment is the final stage of acceptance, but now inspection leads to acceptance. This contradiction introduces uncertainty about when the Buyer is considered to have officially accepted the Deliverables. This impacts warranties and liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 “Delivery” means the completion and/or Delivery of Deliverables in a condition and place acceptable to the Buyer. Acceptance of the Deliverables is considered to have taken place immediately upon delivery and inspection by the Buyer, regardless of whether payment has been effected.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Delivery'. Originally, acceptance was contingent upon payment. The modified text states that acceptance occurs immediately upon delivery and inspection, regardless of payment. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, because the original definition of delivery suggests payment is the final stage of acceptance, but now inspection leads to acceptance as defined in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: 1.1 “Confidential Information” means; all technical, commercial, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, customer lists, investors, employees, business and contractual relationships, business forecasts, sales and merchandising, and marketing plans business or personnel information disclosed or otherwise made available in any format and/or physical manner by Grindrod SA or becoming available, before, during and/or after the execution of an interaction, duty or obligation including all information that makes itself known to the Vendor or comes into being as a result of the rendering, production and/or delivery of an agreement/understanding/request for quotation/contract or Purchase Order, or any other interaction. Confidential Information shall also include any other information that is marked as "Confidential" or should reasonably be considered confidential Confidential Information excludes information which is already in the possession, or under the control of the Vendor otherwise than as a result of having been disclosed by Grindrod SA to the Vendor or as a result of the preparation and execution of a proposal or any other interaction with Grindrod SA. Confidential Information also excludes information in the public domain for a reason, other than a breach of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Undertaking, with any party, or independently developed by the Vendor without reference to information provided by, Grindrod SA;
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 “Confidential Information” means; only that information which is explicitly marked as "Confidential" in writing by Grindrod SA. Confidential Information excludes all other information, including but not limited to, technical, commercial, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing, customer lists, investors, employees, business and contractual relationships, business forecasts, sales and merchandising, and marketing plans business or personnel information disclosed or otherwise made available in any format and/or physical manner by Grindrod SA or becoming available, before, during and/or after the execution of an interaction, duty or obligation including all information that makes itself known to the Vendor or comes into being as a result of the rendering, production and/or delivery of an agreement/understanding/request for quotation/contract or Purchase Order, or any other interaction. Confidential Information also excludes information which is already in the possession, or under the control of the Vendor otherwise than as a result of having been disclosed by Grindrod SA to the Vendor or as a result of the preparation and execution of a proposal or any other interaction with Grindrod SA. Confidential Information also excludes information in the public domain for a reason, other than a breach of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Undertaking, with any party, or independently developed by the Vendor without reference to information provided by, Grindrod SA;
perturbation.explanation: The modification to the definition of "Confidential Information" creates an in-text contradiction. The original definition included a broad range of information, including anything 'reasonably considered confidential.' The changed definition limits it to only information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' in writing. This contradicts later clauses that rely on the broader definition, creating uncertainty about what information the Vendor is actually obligated to protect. Specifically this will create a contradiction later on with clause 3.4 which says to request clarification from Grindrod SA, because if the information isn't marked explicitly, then the vendor doesn't have to follow the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4. to, if the Vendor is in doubt concerning the confidentiality of any information or whether the Vendor is authorized to disclose it to any person, request and abide by any written ruling given by Grindrod SA,
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' in writing. This directly contradicts clause 3.4, which instructs the Vendor to seek clarification from Grindrod SA if they are unsure about the confidentiality of 'any information.' The narrowed definition implies that any unmarked information is not confidential, negating the need for clarification and contradicting the obligation to request guidance per clause 3.4. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the vendor doesn't need to abide by clause 3.4 if the information isn't explicitly marked.

perturbation.original_text: 2. The Parties are aware that Confidential Information may be disclosed or otherwise be made accessible to the Vendor for the purpose of the Vendor fulfilling its obligations / promises or duties arising from the Agreement / understanding / Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order agreed to and accepted by the Vendor.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.  The Parties are aware that Vendor Information may be disclosed or otherwise be made accessible to Grindrod SA for the purpose of Grindrod SA fulfilling its obligations / promises or duties arising from the Agreement / understanding / Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order agreed to and accepted by the Grindrod SA.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Vendor. The modified text changes this to say that Vendor Information may be disclosed to Grindrod SA. This in-text contradiction introduces confusion about which party is disclosing information to whom and for what purpose, as it now implies that the vendor needs to disclose information to Grindrod SA for Grindrod's benefit. This makes the obligations related to the confidentiality, in relation to this clause unclear
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.  The Parties are aware that Vendor Information may be disclosed or otherwise be made accessible to Grindrod SA for the purpose of Grindrod SA fulfilling its obligations / promises or duties arising from the Agreement / understanding / Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order agreed to and accepted by the Grindrod SA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Confidential Information may be disclosed to the Vendor' to 'Vendor Information may be disclosed to Grindrod SA'. The contradicted text restates the changed text verbatim, implying that Vendor Information may be disclosed to Grindrod SA for Grindrod SA's benefit. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it changes who is disclosing what to whom and for what purpose. The original text made Vendor the recipient of confidential information, while the change makes Grindrod SA the recipient of Vendor information. This directly contradicts the original agreement and is an 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, because different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations, regarding which party must disclose information, and for whose benefit, thus creating uncertainty about contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: THIS UNDERTAKING IS TO BE MADE BY ALL PERSONS, NATURAL AND LEGAL, (THE VENDOR) WHO ARE GIVEN ACCESS, FOR WHATEVER PURPOSE, TO ANY INFORMATION, CONSIDERED BY GRINDROD SA, TO BE CONFIDENTIAL (AS DEFINED IN THIS UNDERTAKING). THE OBLIGATION RESTS WITH THE VENDOR TO ENSURE THAT ALL PERSONS (NATURAL AND LEGAL) GIVEN ACCESS TO CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION AND SITE’S (OPERATIONS AND ADMINISTRATIVE FACILITIES), COMPLETE AND FORWARD A COPY OF THIS DOCUMENT TO GRINDROD SA’S HEAD OF PROCUREMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: THIS UNDERTAKING IS TO BE MADE BY ALL PERSONS, NATURAL AND LEGAL, (THE VENDOR) WHO ARE GIVEN ACCESS, FOR WHATEVER PURPOSE, TO ANY INFORMATION, CONSIDERED BY GRINDROD SA, TO BE CONFIDENTIAL (AS DEFINED IN THIS UNDERTAKING). THE OBLIGATION RESTS WITH GRINDROD SA TO ENSURE THAT ALL PERSONS (NATURAL AND LEGAL) GIVEN ACCESS TO CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION AND SITE’S (OPERATIONS AND ADMINISTRATIVE FACILITIES), COMPLETE AND FORWARD A COPY OF THIS DOCUMENT TO GRINDROD SA’S HEAD OF PROCUREMENT.
perturbation.explanation: The change shifts the obligation for ensuring compliance with the undertaking from the Vendor to Grindrod SA. This creates a direct in-text contradiction. The initial statement says the vendor is responsible, but after the modification it states that Grindrod SA is now responsible, so it's unclear who has the duty to collect and submit the document. This confusion could lead to non-compliance and disputes regarding accountability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: THIS UNDERTAKING IS TO BE MADE BY ALL PERSONS, NATURAL AND LEGAL, (THE VENDOR) WHO ARE GIVEN ACCESS, FOR WHATEVER PURPOSE, TO ANY INFORMATION, CONSIDERED BY GRINDROD SA, TO BE CONFIDENTIAL (AS DEFINED IN THIS UNDERTAKING). THE OBLIGATION RESTS WITH GRINDROD SA TO ENSURE THAT ALL PERSONS (NATURAL AND LEGAL) GIVEN ACCESS TO CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION AND SITE’S (OPERATIONS AND ADMINISTRATIVE FACILITIES), COMPLETE AND FORWARD A COPY OF THIS DOCUMENT TO GRINDROD SA’S HEAD OF PROCUREMENT.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by shifting the responsibility for ensuring compliance with the undertaking from 'THE VENDOR' to 'GRINDROD SA'. This directly contradicts the original assignment of responsibility, creating a clear inconsistency within the document. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text in this case, but the original text states that the VENDOR is responsible, while the changed text states that GRINDROD SA is now responsible, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as to who holds the duty to ensure all persons complete and forward the document.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1. to use such Confidential Information only for purposes of performing as required in terms of its obligations / promises or duties arising out of the Agreement / understanding /Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order and for no other purpose whatsoever,
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1. to use such Confidential Information only for purposes of performing as required in terms of its obligations / promises or duties arising out of the Agreement / understanding /Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order and for any other business purpose the Vendor deems beneficial,
perturbation.explanation: The modification allows the Vendor to use Confidential Information for 'any other business purpose the Vendor deems beneficial,' which directly contradicts the original clause that restricts its use to performing obligations under the agreement. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty because if the vendor deems that there is another beneficial purpose for using the Confidential Information, then the clause doesn't restrict the vendor at all, therefore, negating the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1. to use such Confidential Information only for purposes of performing as required in terms of its obligations / promises or duties arising out of the Agreement / understanding /Request for Quotation / Contract or Purchase Order and for any other business purpose the Vendor deems beneficial,
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Vendor to use Confidential Information for 'any other business purpose the Vendor deems beneficial,' which directly contradicts the original clause (and contradicted text, in this case) that restricts its use to performing obligations under the agreement. The original text says the Vendor can ONLY use Confidential Information for specific purposes related to the agreement. The changed text then permits the Vendor to use the same information for ANY purpose they deem beneficial, negating the restrictions outlined in the contradicted clause. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding how the Vendor is allowed to use Confidential Information, as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
